hero.title: Path from 0.0.2 to 0.0.3
hero.subtitle: A language roadmap focused on making MKS more usable as a real scripting and systems-oriented language: stronger stdlib, clearer modules, better tooling, and a cleaner path toward compilation.

pathBadge.label: surface stable, stdlib and tooling next

sections.title: Core language surface
sections.subtitle: The main syntax and runtime-facing language surface already exist. The goal now is to keep this layer understandable and predictable instead of bloating it too early.

cards.title: Variables and expressions
cards.description: MKS already has a readable identity: var bindings, =: assignment, arithmetic, comparisons, and logical flow.

cards.title: Functions and control flow
cards.description: Functions, if/else, while, for, repeat, break, and continue already define how code is written in MKS.

cards.title: Entities and extend
cards.description: Entity and extend already give MKS its object-like and method-oriented style.

sections.title: Standard library growth
sections.subtitle: This is the main 0.0.3 track: make MKS practical enough that people can actually write code in it without fighting the language.

cards.title: Stdlib baseline
cards.description: Turn std into the real daily surface of the language, not just a few wrappers.

cards.title: Bool and data helpers
cards.description: Small helpers should reduce boilerplate and make scripts feel less raw.

cards.title: Package groundwork
cards.description: A real package direction should start small: importable libraries, reusable folders, and a clean convention before a full package manager.

sections.title: Modules and imports
sections.subtitle: Module behavior should feel strict and obvious. Confusing import rules will make the language feel fake fast.

cards.title: Resolver clarity
cards.description: Make import resolution clear enough that users know exactly where code comes from.

cards.title: Alias semantics
cards.description: Do not pretend alias imports are done until runtime and evaluation fully support them.

cards.title: Library structure
cards.description: Reusable MKS code should have a clean file structure before packages become official.

sections.title: Tooling and developer workflow
sections.subtitle: Once the language surface is readable, daily use has to stop feeling raw.

cards.title: Socrates
cards.description: Official formatter for one code style across blocks, entities, extend, loops, and imports.

cards.title: Diagnostics
cards.description: Errors should help fix code faster instead of only stating that something exploded.

cards.title: Test workflow
cards.description: Built-in test blocks should grow into a real lightweight workflow for language users.

sections.title: Runtime consistency and performance
sections.subtitle: Runtime work matters, but it should follow clear language semantics instead of racing ahead of them.

cards.title: Method consistency
cards.description: Native and extended methods should feel like one coherent system.

cards.title: Evaluator speed
cards.description: Speed work only matters once user-facing semantics stop shifting every week.

cards.title: GC visibility
cards.description: The runtime should become easier to reason about when debugging memory and performance.

sections.title: Compilation path
sections.subtitle: Compilation is important, but only when the interpreted language already feels coherent.

cards.title: Bytecode direction
cards.description: Explore whether MKS should first stabilize through bytecode before chasing full native output.

cards.title: Native compiler path
cards.description: Push toward a real native binary path once AST semantics and stdlib expectations are solid.

cards.title: Benchmarks
cards.description: Only meaningful after the compiler/runtime surface stops moving all the time.

sections.title: Systems direction
sections.subtitle: This is the bigger vision: make MKS more capable in OS-facing and lower-level workflows without losing its own identity.

cards.title: OS interaction
cards.description: The language should eventually be able to do more than toy scripting.

cards.title: Package system
cards.description: A package story matters if MKS should grow beyond one-file experiments.

cards.title: Hardware reach
cards.description: Long-term direction for lower-level integrations and system-facing libraries.

timeline.title: Useful language milestones
timeline.subtitle: Each step should make MKS easier to write, understand, or grow.

items.title: Core syntax identity
items.text: Variables, control flow, entities, extend, watch, defer, and import basics are already shaping the language.

items.title: 0.0.3 stdlib track
items.text: Make stdlib practical enough that MKS feels usable for real scripts.

items.title: Tooling layer
items.text: Add MKSFmt and better diagnostics so writing MKS feels less raw.

items.title: Runtime strength
items.text: Method consistency, evaluator speed, and better GC visibility.

items.title: Compiler and systems reach
items.text: Bytecode or native compiler path, package system, OS access, and hardware direction.

principle.title: Make the language feel real before making it huge.
principle.text: MKS should first become predictable, practical, and pleasant to write. More power matters, but only after the surface, stdlib, modules, and tooling stop fighting the user.

versionPath.title: Path 0.0.2 to 0.0.3
versionPath.subtitle: 0.0.2 defines the core identity. 0.0.3 should make MKS more practical through stdlib growth, module clarity, and first real tooling.

items.label: Core identity
items.text: Core syntax, control flow, entities, extend, and runtime-style features define how MKS code feels.

items.label: Practical scripting
items.text: Active work: stdlib growth, import clarity, MKSFmt groundwork, and better diagnostics.

items.label: Module and package shape
items.text: Cleaner module structure, reusable library conventions, and package groundwork.

items.label: Compilation path
items.text: First serious bytecode or native compilation direction.

items.label: Systems reach
items.text: Packages, OS interaction, stronger runtime, and groundwork for lower-level workflows.
